name: torrentool

metadata.name: torrentool
metadata.summary: The tool to work with torrent files.

documentation:
# torrentool

<https://github.com/idlesign/torrentool>

```{image} https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/torrentool.svg
:target: https://pypi.python.org/pypi/torrentool
```

```{image} https://img.shields.io/pypi/l/torrentool.svg
:target: https://pypi.python.org/pypi/torrentool
```

```{image} https://img.shields.io/coveralls/idlesign/torrentool/master.svg
:target: https://coveralls.io/r/idlesign/torrentool
```

## Description

*The tool to work with torrent files.*

Works on Python 3.7+.

Includes:

- Command line interface. Requires `click` package to be installed.

  Use `pip install torrentool[cli]` to install this dependency automatically.

- Torrent utils (file creation, read and modification).

- Bencoding utils (decoder, encoder).

### Using CLI

```bash
; Make .torrent out of `video.mkv`
$ torrentool torrent create /home/my/files_here/video.mkv

; Make .torrent out of entire `/home/my/files_here` dir,
; and put some open trackers announce URLs into it,
; and publish file on torrent caching service, so it is ready to share.
$ torrentool torrent create /home/my/files_here --open_trackers --cache

; Print out existing file info.
$ torrentool torrent info /home/my/some.torrent
```

Use command line `--help` switch to know more.

:::{note}
Some commands require `requests` package to be installed.
:::

### From your Python code

```python
from torrentool.api import Torrent

# Reading and modifying an existing file.
my_torrent = Torrent.from_file('/home/idle/some.torrent')
my_torrent.total_size  # Total files size in bytes.
my_torrent.magnet_link  # Magnet link for you.
my_torrent.comment = 'Your torrents are mine.'  # Set a comment.
my_torrent.to_file()  # Save changes.

# Or we can create a new torrent from a directory.
new_torrent = Torrent.create_from('/home/idle/my_stuff/')  # or it could have been a single file
new_torrent.announce_urls = 'udp://tracker.openbittorrent.com:80'
new_torrent.to_file('/home/idle/another.torrent')
```

classes.name: torrentool.exceptions.TorrentoolException
documentation: Base torrentool exception. All others are inherited from it.

classes.name: torrentool.exceptions.BencodeError
documentation: Base exception for bencode related errors.

classes.name: torrentool.exceptions.BencodeDecodingError
documentation: Raised when torrentool is unable to decode bencoded data.

classes.name: torrentool.exceptions.BencodeEncodingError
documentation: Raised when torrentool is unable to encode data into bencode.

classes.name: torrentool.exceptions.TorrentError
documentation: Base exception for Torrent object related errors.

classes.name: torrentool.exceptions.RemoteUploadError
documentation: Base class for upload to remotes related issues.

classes.name: torrentool.exceptions.RemoteDownloadError
documentation: Base class for issues related to downloads from remotes.

Encodes a value into bencoded bytes.

```

Python object to be encoded (str, int, list, dict).
```

Decodes bencoded data introduced as bytes.

Returns decoded structure(s).

```

param byte_keys

Keys values for which should be treated
as bytes (as opposed to UTF-8 strings).
```

classes.name: torrentool.torrent.TorrentFile
documentation: Represents a file in torrent.

classes.name: torrentool.torrent.Torrent

methods.name: torrentool.torrent.Torrent.webseeds
documentation:
A list of URLs where torrent data can be retrieved.

See also: Torrent.httpseeds

http://bittorrent.org/beps/bep_0019.html

methods.name: torrentool.torrent.Torrent.httpseeds
documentation:
A list of URLs where torrent data can be retrieved.

See also and prefer Torrent.webseeds

http://bittorrent.org/beps/bep_0017.html

methods.name: torrentool.torrent.Torrent.files
documentation:
Files in torrent.

List of namedtuples (filepath, size).

methods.name: torrentool.torrent.Torrent.total_size
documentation: Total size of all files in torrent.

methods.name: torrentool.torrent.Torrent.info_hash
documentation: Hash of torrent file info section. Also known as torrent hash.

methods.name: torrentool.torrent.Torrent.magnet_link
documentation: Magnet link using BTIH (BitTorrent Info Hash) URN.

methods.name: torrentool.torrent.Torrent.announce_urls
documentation:
List of lists of announce (tracker) URLs.

First inner list is considered as primary announcers list,
the following lists as back-ups.

http://bittorrent.org/beps/bep_0012.html

methods.name: torrentool.torrent.Torrent.comment
documentation: Optional. Free-form textual comments of the author.

methods.name: torrentool.torrent.Torrent.source
documentation: Optional. Often used by private trackers to create a unique infohash to prevent peer-leak.

methods.name: torrentool.torrent.Torrent.creation_date
documentation: Optional. The creation time of the torrent, in standard UNIX epoch format. UTC.

methods.name: torrentool.torrent.Torrent.created_by
documentation: Optional. Name and version of the program used to create the .torrent

methods.name: torrentool.torrent.Torrent.private
documentation:
Optional. If True the client MUST publish its presence to get other peers
ONLY via the trackers explicitly described in the metainfo file. If False or is not present,
the client may obtain peer from other means, e.g. PEX peer exchange, dht.

methods.name: torrentool.torrent.Torrent.name
documentation: Torrent name (title).

methods.name: torrentool.torrent.Torrent.get_magnet
documentation:
Returns torrent magnet link, consisting of BTIH (BitTorrent Info Hash) URN
anr optional other information.

```
param detailed

For boolean - whether additional info (such as trackers) should be included.
For iterable - expected allowed parameter names:

source:7: (ERROR/3) Unexpected indentation.

tr - trackers
ws - webseeds
```
